episodes.title: Bill Gates grilled by lawmakers in Epstein investigation
episodes.description: The tech billionaire was questioned in a closed-door session on Capitol Hill for nearly six hours about his relationship with the convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

episodes.title: Former Epstein executive assistant appears before House Oversight Committee
episodes.description:
The convicted sex offender's longtime personal assistant is scheduled to appear before the committee Tuesday morning.

episodes.title: Epstein may have left suicide note: Report
episodes.description:
A federal judge is being asked to unseal a purported suicide note by Jeffrey Epstein that a former cellmate told The New York Times he found in a book after Epstein’s July 2019 suicide attempt.

episodes.description: One month after the arrest of Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor, Sir Mark Rowley says the police force is pushing U.S. officials for unredacted material from the Epstein files.

episodes.title: No more Epstein files to be released: DOJ
episodes.description: The Department of Justice is defending its release of the Jeffrey Epstein files and informing Congress that all documents that were required to be released have been and no more are coming out.

episodes.title: Epstein fallout mounts as Hollywood agent sells agency
episodes.description:
Talent agent Casey Wasserman sold his agency after his name and correspondence with Ghislaine Maxwell surfaced in the Epstein files. ABC News' Olivia Rubin. ABC News' Chief Global Affairs Correspondent Martha Raddatz reports on continued fallout from the Epstein files and the U.S. pressure on Iran.

episodes.title: Fallout after some Epstein files taken offline
episodes.description: The lawmakers behind the law to force the release of all the Epstein files are threatening to try to hold Attorney General Pam Bondi in contempt for not releasing them all by last week's deadline.

episodes.title: DOJ releases limited set of Epstein documents
episodes.description: The Justice Department released tens of thousands of documents Friday, meeting a deadline set by a law signed last month after near-unanimous passage in Congress.

episodes.title: Jeffrey Epstein survivor breaks silence, detailing years of abuse starting at 14
episodes.description:
Marina Lacerda, one of the key people responsible for putting Jeffrey Epstein behind bars, is stepping forward to tell her story, hoping it will inspire other survivors of abuse to do the same.

Sources say Lacerda, 37, was identified in Epstein's 2019 indictment as "Minor-Victim 1" and provided key information that helped prosecutors put the sex offender behind bars.

READ more from GMA: https://tinyurl.com/a2k2hace
